input.command:
GIT_AUTHOR_NAME=Coco GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL=coco@nexus.local git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
laboratory: implement guild fixture engines

Replaces the lab.guild-setup / lab.guild-teardown stubs with real
implementations that build a fresh test guild end-to-end.

Setup (engines/guild-fixture.ts):
  1. `nsg init <guildPath> --name <guildName>`.
  2. Per pin: `nsg --guild-root <path> plugin install
     <name>@<version>` — sequential (npm install isn't concurrency-
     safe; peer/optional dep order matters).
  3. Codex registration via duck-type discovery from
     context.upstream — any upstream yield with codexName + remoteUrl
     (the codex-fixture's yield shape) gets registered in the test
     guild. No manifest-level "this is the codex" declaration needed.
  4. Deep-merge `givens.config` into the test guild's guild.json.
  5. Copy `givens.files[]` entries from absolute sourcePath to
     `<guildPath>/<entry.guildPath>`.
  Fail-loud all-or-nothing: any mid-flight error triggers rm -rf
  rollback of the guild dir and re-throws.

Teardown:
  - Archive-presence safety check (same discipline as codex-
    teardown).
  - rm -rf — tolerant of "doesn't exist".

Defaults:
  - guildName = <slug>-<writId-tail-8> from injected _trial.
  - guildPath = <labHost>/.nexus/laboratory/guilds/<guildName>/
    Lab-host-internal placement, consistent with the codex
    bare-repo's location. Differs from the handoff's
    /workspace/experiments/... default; override via givens.guildPath
    if sanctum-side visibility is wanted.

v1 limitation: file copies require absolute sourcePath (throws on
relative). Manifest-relative resolution would need the manifest
CLI to walk fixture givens at post time and rewrite paths —
deferred until the friction shows up.

27 unit tests: deepMerge edge cases, discoverCodexes shape
tolerance, validation guards, teardown safety + happy path +
tolerance + path override. Subprocess-driven happy path (real
nsg init + plugin install + codex add) is deferred to live
smoke verification rather than unit tests — booting the full
nsg toolchain per test is too expensive.

Concludes click c-momaa03d.

Session: cb9f1554-a5f1-4144-88e9-6170b3cc653b
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -8

content.content:
1	import { z } from 'zod';
2	import { guild } from '@shardworks/nexus-core';
3	import { tool } from '@shardworks/tools-apparatus';
4	import type { ClerkApi } from '../types.ts';
5	
6	/**
7	 * Minimal structural subset of the codexes apparatus's `ScriptoriumApi`
8	 * that the commission-post handler relies on. Declared locally to keep
9	 * the codexes apparatus a soft (`recommends`) dependency — clerk does
10	 * not pull `@shardworks/codexes-apparatus` into its dependency graph.
11	 */
12	interface CodexRegistry {
13	  list(): Promise<Array<{ name: string }>>;
14	}
15	
16	export default tool({
17	  name: 'commission-post',
18	  description: 'Post a new commission, creating a writ in open or new (draft) phase',
19	  instructions:
20	    'Creates a new writ. By default the writ is placed in open phase and enters the queue ' +
21	    'immediately. Pass draft: true to create the writ in new (draft) phase instead — draft ' +
22	    'writs are held out of the queue until explicitly published with writ-publish. ' +
23	    'The writ type must be a type declared in the guild config, or the built-in type "mandate". ' +
24	    'If type is omitted, the guild\'s configured default type is used (defaults to "mandate"). ' +
25	    'Use parentId to create this writ as a child of an existing writ. The parent must be in ' +
26	    'new or open phase. ' +
27	    'When codex is omitted: if parentId is provided, the codex is inherited from the parent ' +
28	    'writ; otherwise, when the guild has exactly one registered codex it is selected ' +
29	    'automatically, when the guild has two or more registered codexes the call fails with ' +
30	    'an error naming the candidates, and when no codexes are registered the call fails ' +
31	    'asking the operator to install or declare one.',
32	  params: {
33	    title: z.string().describe('Short human-readable title describing the work'),
34	    body: z.string().describe('Detail text or description'),
35	    type: z.string().optional().describe('Writ type (default: guild defaultType or "mandate")'),
36	    codex: z
37	      .string()
38	      .optional()
39	      .describe(
40	        'Target codex name. Optional. When omitted: inherited from parentId when provided; ' +
41	        'otherwise defaulted to the single registered codex when the guild has exactly one; ' +
42	        'rejected at post time when the guild has two or more (error names the candidates) or ' +
43	        'when no codexes are registered.',
44	      ),
45	    draft: z
46	      .boolean()
47	      .optional()
48	      .describe(
49	        'When true, create the writ in new (draft) phase instead of open. ' +
50	        'Draft writs must be published before they enter the execution queue.',
51	      ),
52	    parentId: z
53	      .string()
54	      .optional()
55	      .describe(
56	        'Create this writ as a child of the specified parent writ. ' +
57	        'The parent must be in new or open phase.',
58	      ),
59	  },
60	  permission: 'write',
61	  handler: async (params) => {
62	    const clerk = guild().apparatus<ClerkApi>('clerk');
63	    const resolvedParentId = params.parentId
64	      ? await clerk.resolveId(params.parentId)
65	      : undefined;
66	
67	    // Resolve codex up-front when the caller did not supply one and is
68	    // not relying on parent inheritance. Two cases collapse to a hard
69	    // error here so that no codex-less writ enters the queue: a guild
70	    // with multiple registered codexes (ambiguous default) and a guild
71	    // with none (nothing to inherit from). A guild with exactly one
72	    // registered codex defaults silently. When parentId is supplied we
73	    // skip resolution entirely and let clerk.post()'s existing parent-
74	    // inheritance branch fire.
75	    let resolvedCodex = params.codex;
76	    if (resolvedCodex === undefined && resolvedParentId === undefined) {
77	      const codexes = guild().tryApparatus<CodexRegistry>('codexes');
78	      const registered = codexes ? await codexes.list() : [];
79	      const names = registered
80	        .map((entry) => entry.name)
81	        .sort((a, b) => a.localeCompare(b));
82	      if (names.length === 1) {
83	        resolvedCodex = names[0];
84	      } else if (names.length === 0) {
85	        throw new Error(
86	          'no codexes are registered; install a codex package or declare one in guild.json before posting commissions',
87	        );
88	      } else {
89	        throw new Error(
90	          `commission-post: --codex is required when the guild has multiple codexes (registered: ${names.join(', ')})`,
91	        );
92	      }
93	    }
94	
95	    const writ = await clerk.post({
96	      title: params.title,
97	      body: params.body,
98	      type: params.type,
99	      codex: resolvedCodex,
100	      parentId: resolvedParentId,
101	    });
102	
103	    // Auto-publish for mandate writs: `post()` always lands the writ in its
104	    // type's declared initial state, which for mandate is `new` (draft).
105	    // The commission-post tool preserves its prior UX — by default the
106	    // posted writ enters the queue immediately — by transitioning the
107	    // writ to `open` here when the caller did not request a draft. The
108	    // auto-advance is deliberately confined to mandate: for other
109	    // plugin-registered types the initial state is the caller's only
110	    // landing spot, and advancing without a type-specific tool would be
111	    // silent coupling.
112	    if (params.draft !== true && writ.type === 'mandate') {
113	      return clerk.transition(writ.id, 'open');
114	    }
115	    return writ;
116	  },
117	});
118

content.text: OK, design choice for Step 3: I'll implement both engines per the click, but make `lab.commission-post-xguild` capable of inline-waiting (default `waitForTerminal: true`) since the rig template only has one scenario engine slot and the canonical "post and wait" flow needs to fit in one engine. The standalone `lab.wait-for-writ-terminal-xguild` is also useful as a building block. Both share a `waitForWritTerminal` helper.

input.command: nsg click conclude c-momaa1vt --conclusion "Cross-guild scenario engines landed (engines/scenario-xguild.ts). lab.commission-post-xguild posts a commission via 'nsg --guild-root <test-guild> commission-post', then INLINE polls until terminal (default waitForTerminal=true) — single engine fits the rig template's single-scenario-engine slot for the canonical 'post and wait' flow. Optional opt-out via waitForTerminal=false yields just {writId, postedAt}. Test guild discovery via duck-typing on context.upstream (any yield with {guildName, guildPath}). Title defaults to brief's first H1, then 'Commission from <basename>'. Codex auto-resolves in the test guild (single-codex case) or inherits from parentId. lab.wait-for-writ-terminal-xguild is the standalone polling engine — useful for future multi-step scenarios where post is detached from wait. Both engines share waitForWritTerminal helper which polls 'nsg writ-show --format json' and checks classification==='terminal' to detect terminal state. Defaults: pollIntervalMs=5000, timeoutMs=1.8M (30 min), fail-loud on timeout. 22 unit tests covering pure helpers (discoverTestGuilds 5 cases, extractH1Title 6 cases) + engine validation guards (briefPath absolute, no/multi guild, malformed numbers, missing brief, writId required). Subprocess happy path deferred to live smoke verification — same rationale as guild-fixture (booting nsg per test is too expensive). Live-verify pending fresh daemon restart since all three engine pairs (codex, guild, scenario) post-date the daemon's loaded code." 2>&1 | tail -3

- session: cb9f1554-a5f1-4144-88e9-6170b3cc653b

  item: "Third real engine pair lands — lab.commission-post-xguild + lab.wait-for-writ-terminal-xguild (concludes c-momaa1vt). engines/scenario-xguild.ts: commission-post posts via `nsg --guild-root <test-guild> commission-post` and INLINE polls until terminal (default waitForTerminal=true) — single engine fits the rig template's single-scenario-engine slot for the canonical 'post and wait' flow. Considered three architectures (post+wait as two engines chained via graft; one composite engine; post engine with optional wait inline) and picked the third because graftTail doesn't gate downstream-engine starts (only rig-completion), so probes upstream from `scenario` would race with a separate wait engine. wait-for-writ-terminal is the standalone polling engine retained as a building block for future multi-step scenarios. Both engines share a waitForWritTerminal helper that polls `nsg writ-show --format json` checking classification==='terminal'. Defaults: pollIntervalMs=5000, timeoutMs=30 min, fail-loud on timeout. Test guild discovery via duck-typing on context.upstream (any {guildName,guildPath} yield) — same pattern as the codex discovery in guild-fixture. Brief title defaults to first H1, falling back to 'Commission from <basename>'. Codex auto-resolves in the test guild (single-codex case) or inherits from parentId. 22 unit tests: discoverTestGuilds (5 cases mirroring discoverCodexes shape), extractH1Title (6 cases: present/missing/whitespace/H2-skip/multiple-pick-first/empty), commission-post validation (briefPath absolute, no-guild, multi-guild, poll/timeout positive, missing brief), wait-engine validation (writId required/non-empty, no-guild, poll positive). Subprocess happy path deferred to live verification — same rationale as guild-fixture. Three engine pairs (codex, guild, scenario) all unit-tested but unverified live; the daemon predates all this work. Will need a fresh restart for a multi-engine smoke trial that exercises the full chain end-to-end."
  commissionable: false
  justification: sanctum

Test guild discovery via duck-typing on context.upstream (any {guildName,guildPath} yield) — same pattern as the codex discovery in guild-fixture. Brief title defaults to first H1, falling back to 'Commission from <basename>'. Codex auto-resolves in the test guild (single-codex case) or inherits from parentId. 22 unit tests: discoverTestGuilds (5 cases mirroring discoverCodexes shape), extractH1Title (6 cases: present/missing/whitespace/H2-skip/multiple-pick-first/empty), commission-post validation (briefPath absolute, no-guild, multi-guild, poll/timeout positive, missing brief), wait-engine validation (writId required/non-empty, no-guild, poll positive). Subprocess happy path deferred to live verification — same rationale as guild-fixture. Three engine pairs (codex, guild, scenario) all unit-tested but unverified live; the daemon predates all this work. Will need a fresh restart for a multi-engine smoke trial that exercises the full chain end-to-end."

lines: item: "Second real engine pair lands — lab.guild-setup + lab.guild-teardown (concludes c-momaa03d). engines/guild-fixture.ts: setup runs `nsg init <path>` → per-pin `nsg --guild-root <path> plugin install <name>@<version>` (sequential — npm install in same dir doesn't tolerate concurrency, peer/optional deps order-sensitive) → codex registration via duck-typed discovery from context.upstream (any upstream yield with codexName+remoteUrl gets `nsg codex add` in the test guild — no manifest-level dependsOn declaration of 'this is the codex' needed) → deep-merge givens.config into guild.json → copy givens.files entries from absolute sourcePath to <guildPath>/<entry.guildPath>. Fail-loud all-or-nothing rollback (rm -rf the guild dir) on any mid-flight error. Teardown: archive-presence safety check (mirrors codex-teardown discipline) + rm -rf. Auto-defaulting matches codex pattern: guildName defaults to <slug>-<writId-tail-8> from _trial; guildPath defaults to <labHostGuild>/.nexus/laboratory/guilds/<name>/ — lab-host-internal placement consistent with codex bare repo's home. Departs from the handoff's /workspace/experiments/<slug>-<id-prefix>/ default — preferred lab-host-internal for filesystem-cleanup symmetry; can be overridden via givens.guildPath if sanctum-side visibility wins. v1 limitation: file copies require absolute sourcePath (throws on relative); manifest-relative resolution is future polish that would require manifest CLI to walk fixture givens at post time and rewrite paths. 27 unit tests covering deepMerge (5 cases: flat, nested, array replacement, type-mismatch, no-mutation), discoverCodexes (6 cases: empty, single, multiple, mixed-shape upstream, type-rejection, null-tolerance), defaultGuildPath, plus engine validation guards (8 rejections) + teardown safety/happy-path/tolerance/override (4 cases). Subprocess-driven happy path (nsg init / plugin install / codex add) deferred to live smoke testing rather than unit tests — booting the full nsg toolchain per test is too expensive. Live-verify pending fresh daemon restart since the running spider has the pre-edit code loaded; verify manifest staged at .scratch/lab-trial-verify-guild-fixture.yaml ready to post."

GIT_AUTHOR_NAME=Coco GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL=coco@nexus.local git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
laboratory: implement cross-guild scenario engines

Replaces the lab.commission-post-xguild / lab.wait-for-writ-
terminal-xguild stubs with real implementations.

The rig template only has a single scenario-engine slot, so
`lab.commission-post-xguild` posts AND inline-polls until
terminal by default (waitForTerminal=true). Considered three
shapes — chained via graft, fused composite, post+inline-wait
— and picked inline-wait because graftTail only gates rig
completion, not downstream-engine starts. Probes upstream
from `scenario` would race with a separate wait engine.

`lab.wait-for-writ-terminal-xguild` is retained as the
standalone polling building block — useful for future
multi-step scenarios where post is detached from wait.

Both engines share a `waitForWritTerminal` helper that polls
`nsg writ-show --format json` and checks classification ===
'terminal'. Defaults: pollIntervalMs=5000, timeoutMs=30min,
fail-loud on timeout.

Test-guild discovery mirrors the codex-discovery pattern in
guild-fixture: duck-type any upstream yield with both
{guildName, guildPath}. Brief title defaults to the first H1,
then to "Commission from <basename>".

22 unit tests:
  - discoverTestGuilds (5 cases),
  - extractH1Title (6 cases),
  - validation guards on both engines.

Subprocess happy path deferred to live smoke verification —
same rationale as guild-fixture.

Three engine pairs (codex, guild, scenario) are now real and
unit-tested. Daemon predates all of them; needs a fresh
restart for a multi-engine smoke trial.

Concludes click c-momaa1vt.

Session: cb9f1554-a5f1-4144-88e9-6170b3cc653b
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -8

`cc83efd2` committed. Three engine pairs done.

## Status — Steps 1–3 complete

| Engine pair | Click | Status | Live-verified? |
|---|---|---|---|
| `lab.codex-{setup,teardown}` | `c-moma9y1k` | ✅ committed | ✅ basic flow (explicit codexName) |
| `lab.guild-{setup,teardown}` | `c-momaa03d` | ✅ committed | ❌ stale daemon |
| `lab.commission-post-xguild` + `lab.wait-for-writ-terminal-xguild` | `c-momaa1vt` | ✅ committed | ❌ stale daemon |

123/123 tests pass, 7 commits in this session, all 3 unblocked engine pairs done.

## What's next + a natural pause point

Steps 4–5 (probes + archive engine) are gated on the **archive-design conversation** in the companion handoff (`c-momaa5o9`). That's a separate thread — different mental shape, different scope — and the right next move once we want to unblock those.

Steps 6–10 are post-implementation cleanups:
- 6: Smoke test as a package test (codify the live-verify pattern)
- 7: Retire the superseded infra spec
- 8: Documentation
- 9: First real-world trial port

**My take:** good place to pause. Three productive things you could do next, depending on what you want:

1. **Restart the daemon and verify the full chain end-to-end** — I have the staged manifest at `.scratch/lab-trial-verify-guild-fixture.yaml`. Quick ~2 minute confirmation that all three engine pairs cooperate.
2. **Pivot to the archive-design conversation** (companion handoff). Unblocks probes + archive, which are the largest remaining unimplemented piece.
3. **Just call it.** Pick this thread back up later.

What's the call?

  item: "Third real engine pair lands — lab.commission-post-xguild + lab.wait-for-writ-terminal-xguild (concludes c-momaa1vt). engines/scenario-xguild.ts: commission-post posts via `nsg --guild-root <test-guild> commission-post` and INLINE polls until terminal (default waitForTerminal=true) — single engine fits the rig template's single-scenario-engine slot for the canonical 'post and wait' flow. Considered three architectures (post+wait as two engines chained via graft; one composite engine; post engine with optional wait inline) and picked the third because graftTail doesn't gate downstream-engine starts (only rig-completion), so probes upstream from `scenario` would race with a separate wait engine. wait-for-writ-terminal is the standalone polling engine retained as a building block for future multi-step scenarios. Both engines share a waitForWritTerminal helper that polls `nsg writ-show --format json` checking classification==='terminal'. Defaults: pollIntervalMs=5000, timeoutMs=30 min, fail-loud on timeout. Test guild discovery via duck-typing on context.upstream (any {guildName,guildPath} yield) — same pattern as the codex discovery in guild-fixture. Brief title defaults to first H1, falling back to 'Commission from <basename>'. Codex auto-resolves in the test guild (single-codex case) or inherits from parentId. 22 unit tests: discoverTestGuilds (5 cases mirroring discoverCodexes shape), extractH1Title (6 cases: present/missing/whitespace/H2-skip/multiple-pick-first/empty), commission-post validation (briefPath absolute, no-guild, multi-guild, poll/timeout positive, missing brief), wait-engine validation (writId required/non-empty, no-guild, poll positive). Subprocess happy path deferred to live verification — same rationale as guild-fixture. Three engine pairs (codex, guild, scenario) all unit-tested but unverified live; the daemon predates all this work. Will need a fresh restart for a multi-engine smoke trial that exercises the full chain end-to-end."

- session: cb9f1554-a5f1-4144-88e9-6170b3cc653b
  date: 2026-05-01
  item: "Live verification of the three implemented engine pairs against vibers (after Sean restarted the daemon). Two trials posted and completed. Trial 1 (w-momj4qcu, rig rig-momj4tf8-1702153d, 14s wallclock): codex auto-default → guild-setup with plugin install (tools+codexes via file:<path> npm syntax — link: is pnpm-only and trips EUNSUPPORTEDPROTOCOL on npm guilds) → codex registration in test guild → stub scenario+archive → teardown chain. Codex name auto-derived as `verify-fixtures-a5d9ea57` (slug + writId tail-8). Trial 2 (w-momj78c5, rig rig-momj7a34-139e5044, 13.5s wallclock): same setup PLUS clerk+stacks plugins, then real lab.commission-post-xguild posting a brief to the test guild with waitForTerminal=false, then teardown. Both trials ran 11 engines each in single attempts. Verified end-to-end: _trial injection (slug+writId+fixtureId via phases.ts at graft time), codex auto-default, guild auto-default, codex registration via duck-typed upstream discovery (any {codexName, remoteUrl} yield gets `nsg codex add` in the test guild), test-guild discovery via {guildName, guildPath} duck-typing in commission-post-xguild, plugin install loop (sequential, file:<path> for npm-mode link installs), commission-post shell-out + JSON parse, teardown chain order (guild-teardown before codex-teardown via reverse topo). Not live-verified but unit-test-covered: waitForTerminal=true polling path, standalone wait-for-writ-terminal-xguild engine, deep-merge guild.json config, file copies. Two pieces of grit surfaced: (1) Failed trials leave orphan codexes — when guild-setup fails after codex-setup succeeds, the rig fails without running teardowns, leaving the codex registered in the lab-host's guild.json. Worth a follow-up click — teardown should still run on rig failure to clean up upstream side effects, OR setup engines should grab a release-on-rig-terminal handle. (2) Daemon's Scriptorium has stale in-memory state vs guild.json after CLI-side `nsg codex remove` invocations — the CLI runs in a separate process with its own Scriptorium that updates guild.json, but the daemon's in-memory state isn't notified, and the daemon's next guild.json write reverts the orphans. Cosmetic for now (orphan codexes don't run any code), but worth flagging."
  commissionable: false
  justification: sanctum

content.text:
## Done — three engine pairs implemented and live-verified

```
62abff2d  coco-log: live verify of the three laboratory engine pairs
cc83efd2  laboratory: implement cross-guild scenario engines        ← Step 3
f93266bc  laboratory: implement guild fixture engines                ← Step 2
5b4b71b8  laboratory: implement codex fixture engines (local-bare)   ← Step 1
```

**Two trials posted against vibers, both cleanly completed:**
| Trial | Engines | Wallclock | Coverage |
|---|---|---|---|
| `w-momj4qcu` (fixture chain) | 11 / 11 | 14s | codex+guild auto-defaults, _trial injection, codex registration, teardown chain |
| `w-momj78c5` (scenario chain) | 11 / 11 | 13.5s | + stacks/clerk plugin install, commission-post-xguild shell-out + JSON parse |

## Two pieces of grit worth follow-up clicks (your call)

1. **Failed-trial orphans.** When guild-setup fails after codex-setup succeeds, teardowns don't run — codex stays registered. Setup engines should either release on rig-terminal, or teardown should always run on failure to clean upstream side effects.

2. **Daemon vs CLI Scriptorium state drift.** `nsg codex remove` from the CLI runs in a separate process; updates guild.json, but the daemon's in-memory state isn't notified, and the daemon's next guild.json write reverts the orphans. Cosmetic (orphan bare repos don't run code) but means CLI-side cleanup of orphans doesn't stick across daemon writes.

Vibers' guild.json currently has three orphan codexes (`verify-fixtures-1267530e/2244ce82/40dd2663`) that will keep coming back until the daemon is restarted. They don't break anything, just clutter `nsg codex list` and force `--codex` on subsequent `lab-trial-post` calls.

## Where we are on the handoff

| Step | Status |
|---|---|
| 1. Codex fixture engines | ✅ shipped + live-verified |
| 2. Guild fixture engines | ✅ shipped + live-verified |
| 3. Cross-guild scenario engines | ✅ shipped + live-verified (post path; polling path covered by unit tests only) |
| 4. *(Archive design — companion handoff)* | 🚧 blocked on archive-design conversation |
| 5. Probes | 🚧 blocked on (4) |
| 6. Codified smoke test | ⏳ next mechanical step |
| 7. Retire infra spec | ⏳ docs/cleanup |
| 8. Documentation | ⏳ docs |
| 9. First real-world trial | ⏳ capstone |

Natural pause point. Steps 4–5 require the archive-design conversation. Steps 6–9 are mechanical. Want me to keep going on Step 6 (codified smoke test), pivot to one of the grit follow-ups above, or call it for the session?
